# The Shattered Armor

> Healing Complex Trauma Without the Textbook

## About
The Shattered Armor is a content site focused on C-PTSD recovery, nervous system regulation, and trauma healing. Written by Kalesh, a consciousness teacher and writer whose work explores the intersection of ancient contemplative traditions and modern neuroscience.

## Author
- Name: Kalesh
- Title: Consciousness Teacher & Writer
- Website: https://kalesh.love

## Topics Covered
- Polyvagal theory and nervous system regulation
- Somatic experiencing and body-based trauma recovery
- Internal Family Systems (IFS) and parts work
- Emotional flashbacks and trigger management
- Post-traumatic growth and integration
- Vedantic perspectives on trauma as stored energy
- Embodiment practices and breathwork

## Categories
- The Wiring: How trauma rewires the nervous system
- The Body: Somatic experiencing and embodiment practices
- The Parts: Internal Family Systems and parts work
- The Triggers: Emotional flashbacks and hypervigilance
- The Return: Integration and post-traumatic growth

## Key Researchers Referenced
- Pete Walker (C-PTSD framework)
- Bessel van der Kolk (The Body Keeps the Score)
- Stephen Porges (Polyvagal Theory)
- Peter Levine (Somatic Experiencing)
- Janina Fisher (Structural Dissociation)
- Pat Ogden (Sensorimotor Psychotherapy)
- Dick Schwartz (Internal Family Systems)
- Jiddu Krishnamurti (Consciousness inquiry)
- Alan Watts (Eastern philosophy)
- Sam Harris (Consciousness and meditation)

## Content
This site contains 300 long-form articles (2,500-2,800 words each) on complex trauma recovery. Content is educational and informational — not therapy.

## Contact
Visit https://kalesh.love
